amd_sampled_run.shape: 8xMI350, S4096, GBS128, MBS2, GA8, FSDP8, EP8, no activation checkpointing
amd_sampled_run.mode: MORI/AITER route-mask helper execute, hot_experts=2, min_helper_rows=512, hot_split_w13=true
amd_sampled_run.reason_stopped: sampled timing still made the run too intrusive for a useful two-step throughput metric; rank-0 structured logs showed repeated ~56s fwd/bwd microbatch spans

amd_mori_aiter_hot_execute_tail_half_ran.notes: Tail half over sampled forward hot-helper records; excludes first-use warmup outliers.

amd_mori_aiter_hot_weight_backward_tail_.notes: Tail half by hot-weight label; each label has 16 sampled backward calls per rank.

amd_standard_ep_hot_weight_reference.shape: 8xMI350, S4096, GBS128, MBS4, GA4, FSDP8, EP8, standard EP, helper execute top-8
amd_standard_ep_hot_weight_reference.read: The approximately 231 ms/step backward hot-weight number is the helper partial dW all-reduce itself. The approximately 4.7 ms number is the owner-side scatter over two steps, not the rest of the hot-helper path.

ascend_profile_summary_rankmax_s.source: Remote CSV summaries under /data2/sonle/dsv4_ascend910_fit_20260601/profiles on ascend-910-dev.

read.mori_hot_weight_reduction: Helper partial dW reduction is implemented. On the MORI/AITER sampled W13 path, tail-half hot-weight all-reduce is about 33.2 ms for 16 W13 calls and 11.7 ms for 16 W2 calls per rank; owner scatter is about 2.6 ms and 1.3 ms respectively. The larger remaining MORI/AITER wall is not missing partial reduction, but repeated hot-helper plan/mask/broadcast/sort plus unfused owner local VJP and combine waiting.
read.ascend_moe_ep: Ascend target-batch profiles still show EP alltoallv as a leading communication bucket. Debug force-balance materially reduces alltoallv in the matching 8-layer target-batch profile and in the 12-layer low-batch true-compile profile, but it is not valid learned routing. Its remaining leading buckets shift toward FSDP reduce-scatter, all-gather, AdamW, MatMulV3, SparseAttnSharedkvGrad, and GroupedMatmul.
read.sdpa: PyTorch SDPA means PyTorch scaled_dot_product_attention dispatching to PyTorch ROCm kernels. For the active dense MLA D512 contract, measured PyTorch SDPA fwd+bwd remains faster than the available AITER path; AITER helps D128 controls but not D512, and AITER CK rejects head_dim >256.
read.hipkittens: Current HipKittens evidence means the D512 row-term/recompute producer is already slower than the Triton C4 source-boundary gate before production dQ, dOriKV, route-aware dCmpKV, or dSinks are added. HipKittens remains useful as a schedule/template, but the current branch has not beaten Triton on the replay/recompute part of the full D512 boundary.
